revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:順信]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:順信]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:順信]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:順信]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|1250|4|13}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|religion={{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|qid=Q912221|1=Jōdo Shinshū}}}}
== Junshin (順信) ==
'''Junshin''' (順信, ? – {{ill|Kenchō|en|Kenchō|qid=Q1194270|lt=Kenchō|1=Kenchō}} 2, {{ill|March 10|en|March 10|qid=Q2397|lt=March 10 (lunar calendar)|1=March 10}} ({{ill|1250|en|1250|qid=Q5487|lt=1250|1=1250}} {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|qid=Q2498|lt=April 13|1=April 13}})) was a mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} monk of {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|en|Jōdo Shinshū|qid=Q912221|lt=Jōdo Shinshū|1=Jōdo Shinshū}}. He was one of the direct disciples of {{ill|Shinran|en|Shinran|qid=Q512934|lt=Shinran|1=Shinran}} and is listed among the {{ill|Twenty-Four Disciples|ja|二十四輩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Four Disciples|1=Nijūyohai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11371386}}, Ranking third. His secular name was **Kataoka Nobuhiro** (片岡信広).
== Biography ==
Junshin's father, **Kataoka Nobuchika** (片岡信親), was the chief priest (大宮司) of **Kashima Shrine** (鹿島明神, now {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|qid=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}}) in {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|qid=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}}. He converted to Shinran's teachings and made Junshin his disciple. The **former sites** of Junshin’s teachings include {{ill|Muryōju-ji (Hokota City, Tosu)|ja|無量寿寺 (鉾田市鳥栖)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muryōju-ji (Hokota City, Tosu)|1=Muryojyuji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q110013516}} (belonging to the **Honganji faction**) and {{ill|Muryōju-ji (Hokota City, Shimotomita)|ja|無量寿寺 (鉾田市下冨田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muryōju-ji (Hokota City, Shimotomita)|1=Muryojyuji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q115432910}} (part of the **Otani faction**).
In his later years, under Shinran’s directive, Junshin preached in the Kansai region. The temple **Komyozan Busshō-ji** (光明山佛照寺) in {{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|en|Ibaraki, Osaka|qid=Q502403|lt=Ibaraki, Osaka|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, is recognized as a site associated with his teachings.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} * {{ill|Hongwanji Publishing|ja|本願寺出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongwanji Publishing|1=Honganji Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11521052}}, ''Shinran Shonin Kantō Gokyūseki Guide'' (親鸞聖人関東ご旧跡ガイド), ISBN 4894167204
